items.title: Second case of H5 bird flu detected on South Australian coast

children.content: South Australia has recorded its second confirmed detection of H5 bird flu, in a giant petrel found on the state’s Yorke Peninsula.

gallery.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

thumbnail.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says

children.content: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

image.altText: A factory and furnace against the backdrop of sunset and a lake in the distance

items.title: Teen SA country footballer forced to find new sport after eight concussions

children.content: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

image.altText: a young man holds a red AFL football staring at camera

items.title: Algae which caused brevetoxins in SA algal bloom most toxic of its kind studied

children.content: The dominant species in the algal bloom which devastated SA's coastline,
Karenia cristata
, is the most toxic of its kind studied, according to newly published research.

image.altText: Dead marine life at Adelaide's West Beach.

items.title: Hikers warned to take 'gnarly' peri-urban parks seriously after rescue

children.content: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

image.altText: Young man pulls a face at a plaque with a view of gorge in the background

items.title: Teens reported over 'fight club' as SA Police consider new laws

children.content: SA Police has reported three teenagers for allegedly fighting in a public place after amateur children's "fight club" videos were posted online, while the state's police commissioner says new laws could be introduced to make similar bouts harder to organise.

gallery.altText: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens is standing and wearing a full navy blue police uniform including police hat

thumbnail.altText: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens is standing and wearing a full navy blue police uniform including police hat

items.title: Outback becomes wildlife oasis after 'unbelievable' desert rains

children.content: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.

image.altText: A tiny golden bird nestled among dry grass in what looks to be fairly dry country.

items.title: 'Faeces, urine and dirt': Piggery owners in court on animal cruelty charges

children.content: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

image.altText: A man has short grey hair, he wears a dark jumper and walks outside a brick court building surrounded by reporters

items.title: Fishers buoyed as algal bloom-related whiting ban lifted early

children.content: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

image.altText: Two King George whiting in hands of a man.

items.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

items.title: Farmer celebrates lamb birth boom as twins and triplets thrive

children.content: A wet start to 2026 in regional South Australia has helped create perfect conditions for multiple lamb births on one merino sheep stud.

image.altText: A man with a red hat is smiling holding two black and white speckled sheep.

items.title: SA childcare centre closes for three months after 'very serious' allergy incident

children.content: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to
close immediately for the second time this year.

image.altText: A childcare centre on a quiet street

items.title: 'We spoke for Palestine': Egypt fans on historic World Cup after heartbreaking loss

children.content: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

articles.title: Quick hits: Belgium trolls Trump as Egypt fires up over 'fixed' World Cup

children.content: An all-time blow-up from Egypt's coach headlines the day at the World Cup, as the fallout from Belgium's win over the US continues. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: 'Perversion of justice': Politicians launch bid to investigate FIFA boss

articles.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight

children.content: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout.

items.title: What time is the State of Origin Game III decider tonight?

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

items.title: Qld business fined $1,200 after sparking million-dollar fire ant scare

children.content: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

items.title: Critical drugs could be pulled from PBS amid pricing stoush

children.content: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.
